---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<!-- HERO — Cloud sunset sky -->
<section class="hero-section">

  <!-- Soft cloud texture overlay -->
  <div style="position:absolute;inset:0;background:radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 40%, rgba(253,246,238,0.35) 0%, transparent 60%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 75% 60%, rgba(200,160,200,0.25) 0%, transparent 55%);pointer-events:none;"></div>

  <!-- Hero content -->
  <div class="hero-content">
    <img
      src="images/roo-illustration.png"
      alt="Roo illustration"
      class="hero-illustration"
      onerror="this.style.display='none'"
    />

    <h1 class="hero-name">Roo</h1>
    <p class="hero-tagline">soft mind, sharp thinking</p>

    <div class="hero-pills">
      <span class="hero-pill">☁️ fintech</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">🔍 risk & aml</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">👀 data stories</span>
      <span class="hero-pill">✨ tech + aesthetics</span>
    </div>

    <p class="hero-scroll">scroll to explore ↓</p>
  </div>

</section>

<!-- INTRO -->
<div class="site-section" style="margin-top:4rem;margin-bottom:0;padding-bottom:0;">
  <div style="max-width:600px;">
    <p class="section-eyebrow">Hello</p>
    <h2 class="section-title" style="margin-bottom:1rem;">I'm Roopa — Roo.</h2>
    <p style="font-size:0.92rem;color:#4a5a6a;line-height:2;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">
      Masters student at SMU, with an interest in
      <strong style="color:#2d3a4a;font-weight:500;">understanding financial crime         and studying the intersection of technology,finance, and design</strong>.
    </p>
    <p style="font-size:0.92rem;color:#4a5a6a;line-height:2;">
      I like to build things that are impactful and beautiful. Data tells us many          stories, it is a matter of perspective and that is what I like to explore.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- PROJECTS -->
<div class="site-section">

  <p class="section-eyebrow">Independent Work · Fintech & Financial Crime</p>
  <h2 class="section-title">Projects</h2>

  <p style="font-size:0.88rem;color:#7a8a9a;margin-bottom:2.5rem;line-height:1.9;">
    Self-directed explorations across AML typologies, risk analytics, and emerging
    areas in financial crime — built to go beyond the classroom and demonstrate
    genuine curiosity about where the field is heading.
  </p>
  
  <div class="cards-grid">

    <a href="projects/art-pep/index.html" class="course-card">
      <div class="card-emoji">🎨</div>
      <div class="card-title">Art Markets & the PEP Problem</div>
      <div class="card-desc">
        The art market's opacity makes it a documented vehicle for laundering — 
        especially among politically exposed persons. This project combines auction 
        data, ICIJ offshore leaks, and PEP network analysis to visualise how money 
        and influence move through high-value cultural assets.
      </div>
      <span class="card-tag">R · Tableau · ICIJ · AML</span>
    </a>

    <a href="projects/tbml/index.html" class="course-card">
      <div class="card-emoji">🚢</div>
      <div class="card-title">Trade-Based Money Laundering</div>
      <div class="card-desc">
        TBML is one of the most significant and least visible ML typologies — 
        manipulating trade invoices to move value across borders. Using UN Comtrade 
        mirror trade data, this project surfaces discrepancies across high-risk 
        corridors and maps them to FATF and MAS typology frameworks.
      </div>
      <span class="card-tag">Python · UN Comtrade · FATF</span>
    </a>

    <a href="projects/crypto/index.html" class="course-card">
      <div class="card-emoji">🔗</div>
      <div class="card-title">Crypto & Financial Crime</div>
      <div class="card-desc">
        An honest exploration of on-chain analytics and the evolving regulatory 
        landscape for virtual assets. Part research, part hands-on — tracing 
        transaction flows, understanding mixer patterns, and mapping where 
        MAS and FATF guidance currently stands.
      </div>
      <span class="card-tag">Python · Etherscan · Research</span>
    </a>

  </div>

</div>
```
